items.title: Lethal Kinetic Strike, May 5, 2026
items.description: On May 5, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations. Intelligence confirmed the vessel was transiting along known narco-trafficking routes in the Eastern Pacific and was engaged in narco-trafficking operations. Three male narco-terrorists were killed during this action. No U.S. military forces were harmed.

items.title: NMRTC Pensacola Sailor Spotlight: HM3 Blake Molinaro Protects Unaccompanied Child on the Interstate
items.description: A Naval Hospital Pensacola Sailor, Hospital Corpsman Blake Molinaro, is being recognized for protecting a child who wandered into traffic near Interstate 10 in September 2025. After spotting the child, Molinaro used his vehicle to block traffic while his wife safely guided the child out of the roadway and he contacted authorities. His quick, calm response—shaped by Navy training and medical experience—kept the child safe until deputies arrived, reflecting the Navy’s core values of honor, courage...

items.title: MCRD San Diego Mike Company Graduation
items.description: The new Marines of Mike Company, 3rd Recruit Training Battalion, graduated from Marine Corps Recruit Depot San Diego, May 01 2026. Graduation took place at the completion of the 13-week transformation which included training for drill, marksmanship, basic combat skills, and Marine Corps customs and traditions. Following graduation, the Marines were given 10 days of leave before taking the next step in training at the School of Infantry at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California.
items.unit_name: Marine Corps Recruit Depot, San Diego

items.title: Marine Promoted to Gunnery Sgt. After Defeating Cancer
items.description: MARINE CORPS BASE CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C.– The then Staff Sgt. Alejandro Abrego, an assistant supply chief with 2d Marine Expeditionary Brigade, promoted to his current rank of Gunnery Sergeant May 1, 2026.
items.location_display: Camp Lejeune, North Carolina, United States

items.title: Navy Expeditionary Forces Advance Joint Refueling Capability for Distributed Operations
items.description: NAS JACKSONVILLE, Fla.- Navy Expeditionary Warfighting Development Center (NEXWDC) and Navy Expeditionary Logistics Support Group forces conducted a Limited Objective Experiment (LOE) at Naval Air Station Jacksonville, Florida, from April 21 to May 1, 2026, to evaluate expeditionary refueling solutions supporting P-8A Poseidon operations in an austere environment.
items.location_display: Naval Air Station Jacksonville, Florida, United States

items.title: Bridges of valor: U.S.-Australia partnership delivers EW capability
items.description: U.S. Navy and Royal Australian Air Force personnel gathered April 23 to commemorate Anzac Day, marking a century-old sacrifice that now anchors 12 years of daily partnership in electronic warfare operations at Naval Air Warfare Center Weapons Division.
items.unit_name: Naval Air Warfare Center Weapons Division
items.location_display: Point Mugu Nawc, California, United States

items.title: Surface Team One Launches Revitalized Program for the Fleet
items.description: Surface Team One (ST1) officially launched its revitalized program at Commander, Naval Surface Force (CNSF), with an emphasis on equipping elite project teams with the tools they need to deliver mission-ready ships - on time, ready to fight.
items.unit_name: Commander, Naval Surface Force, U.S. Pacific Fleet

items.title: Change of Command Ensures Continued Mission Success for MSC Atlantic
items.description: Norfolk, Va. (May 1, 2026) – Military Sealift Command Atlantic (MSC Atlantic) welcomed a new leader today as U.S. Navy Capt. Elizabeth A. Nelson took command from U.S. Navy Capt. James A. (Jamie) Murdock at a change of command ceremony held at Vista Point, Naval Station Norfolk, May 1.

items.title: Fifteen years young – Rubidium Fountains continue service to the Navy’s Master Clock
items.description: Rubidium fountain atomic clocks, originally developed at the U.S. Naval Observatory, have met the benchmark of 15 years of operation and continue their contributions as part of the U.S. Naval Observatory's Master Clock ensemble.
items.location_display: Washington, District of Columbia, United States

items.title: U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command/U.S. 4th Fleet Completes FLEX in Key West
items.description: KEY WEST, Fla. – U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command / U.S. 4th Fleet (USNAVSOUTH/4th Fleet) successfully concluded its annual Fleet Experimentation (FLEX) event from April 24-30. The exercise showcased the powerful integration of unmanned systems and artificial intelligence in the fight against transnational organized crime.
items.unit_name: U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command / U.S. 4th Fleet

items.title: Shipyard Spotlight: Dexter Swain, Code 900R.2 Production Resources Department, Production Controller
items.description: Just like his favorite Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes, Dexter Swain, Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY) Code 900R.2 Production Resources Department Production Controller, uses his experience to strategize and navigate his teammates of essential skilled laborers to mission success. Swain has been at NNSY for 13 years and started as a pipefitter. Over the years, he advanced to become a Nuclear Supervisor before being promoted to his current position as Production Controller.
